welcome.nameSubtitle: No account needed — just so we can address you by name in greetings. You can skip it.

mcp.title: MCP Servers
mcp.introPre: Model Context Protocol servers that clients (Claude Desktop, Cursor, …) can launch to talk to the legislation. The configuration is saved in
mcp.emptyPre: You haven't added any yet. Press
mcp.emptyPost: to paste the configuration exactly as you have it in Claude Desktop.
mcp.dialogIntroEnd: You can include several servers at once.
mcp.missingKey: Missing the "mcpServers" key or it isn't an object.
mcp.noneAdded: No server could be added. Check the details in the alerts.

wizard.redetectHint: If you're going to use a local model, start Ollama and press Re-detect. Either way you can pick a cloud model in the next step.
wizard.cloudKeyInstructions: To use it, set the environment variable with your Anthropic API key (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) before launching LexFlow. The key stays on your machine.
wizard.cloudCreateKey: If you don't have one yet, you can create it at console.anthropic.com.
wizard.pullBackgroundHint: You can close the wizard and come back later — the download keeps running in Ollama in the background.
wizard.downloadIntroPost: ({{size}} GB) and install it in Ollama. We'll let you know when it's done.
wizard.ollamaNotRunning: Ollama isn't running. Start it and press Install.

help.label: Help
help.tutorialBody: The 6-step tour walks through the layout, shortcuts and main sections. You can launch it again any time.

home.title: Home
home.description: Your starting point. It summarises the latest published laws, the most active topics and suggested graphs. Use the palette (Ctrl K) to jump to any law from here.

explorer.title: Explorer
explorer.description: Full list of laws with filters by rank, status, jurisdiction and tags. Each row opens the detail view; filters combine with AND.

law.title: Law detail
law.description: Consolidated text of the law with per-article anchors, hierarchy on the left and outgoing references on the right. Change the reading size in Settings → Appearance.

graph.title: Reference graph
graph.description: Each node is a law; edges are citations, amendments or repeals between laws. Drag to pan, scroll to zoom; the right panel sets the exploration depth.

chat.title: Legal chat
chat.description: Chat with a model (local or cloud) that has access to the law corpus. Answers come with citations; each citation is clickable and opens the law in the side panel.

dashboards.title: Dashboards
dashboards.description: Aggregated dashboards over the corpus: distribution by rank, amendment pace, jurisdictions. Useful for an overview before diving into a specific law.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Configure personalization (name, language), appearance, chat models, MCP servers and data. Everything persists locally; no account.

fallback.title: LexFlow
fallback.description: Open source platform to explore and analyze Spanish legislation. Use the command palette (Ctrl K) to jump anywhere.

chat.startHintPre: Type your question below. Cite an article with

lawDetail.selectCitation: Select a citation in the text (superscripts) to see the cited law and its context.

diff.noChanges: No articles changed in this version range.

search.indexing: Indexing the corpus for the first time. Results will appear shortly…

empty.title: No results
empty.description: Try using fewer words or removing accents.

step1.title: Choose your theme
step1.body: You can switch between light and dark at any time with

step2.title: Chat model
step2.body: Pick a default model. Local ones work without Internet; cloud ones require an API key.

step3.title: Telemetry
step3.body: Optional. If you enable it, we'll send anonymous usage metrics (no query text) to improve the product.
step3.notSentBody: We never send the text of your searches or the contents of your chats. Only: which screens you use, how many articles you open per session, and errors.

settings.title: Settings
settings.underConstruction: Section pending — will connect to the matching endpoint.

diagnostics.subtitle: Live process state: memory, disk, corpus, chat DB. Auto-refreshes every 30 s.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.subtitle: LexFlow can log anonymous usage (page views, palette commands, errors) to a local file — never sent to a remote server. Off by default: there are two gates and both must be ON for anything to be recorded.
privacy.toggleHint: Your consent lives in this browser. If the backend operator hasn't enabled persistence, this toggle is informational only — events won't be written even when ON.
privacy.whatWeCollectBody: Pages you visit inside the app, commands you fire from the palette, and unhandled errors. Never the content of your searches, laws, or chat conversations. The log is a daily JSONL file on your local disk; inspect or delete it any time.

toast.optedInBody: Anonymous usage will be recorded from now on (when the operator allows it too).
toast.optedOutBody: We'll stop recording usage from this browser.

personalization.title: Personalization
personalization.subtitle: How the app addresses you, interface language, and a shortcut to accessibility options. No account — everything lives in this browser.
personalization.nameHint: No account needed. Saved only in this browser. Leave it empty and save to go back to nameless greetings.
personalization.languageHint: Greetings, law names and the corpus stay in Spanish — they're legal content, not UI.
personalization.a11yBody: Theme (light / dark), table density and reading size live under Appearance. They'll move here once we have a wider pack (high contrast, motion-reduce, focus improvements).

toast.nameSavedBody: We'll greet you as {{name}} from now on.
toast.nameClearedBody: Greetings will show without your name again.

models.title: Models
models.subtitle: Configure the models available for chat. Local ones run on your machine; cloud ones make outbound requests.

data.title: Data
data.subtitlePre: The corpus syncs from the repository
data.subtitlePost: . It keeps the original Markdown files.

help.title: Help
help.subtitle: Getting-started resources. The guided tutorial walks you through the five product sections in under a minute.
help.welcomeTourSteps: 6 steps: layout, shortcuts, command palette, search, graph and chat.
help.shortcutGoTo: jump to Home / Explorer / Graph / Chat / Dashboards / Settings

searchHelp.title: How to search the Explorer
searchHelp.freetext: Type words from the title or the BOE identifier to filter by text.
searchHelp.tags: Use #tag inside the search (e.g. "#labor dismissal") to filter by topic, Obsidian-style.
searchHelp.filters: The left-rail filters (type, status, jurisdiction, tags) combine with logical AND.

empty.title: No results for that search
empty.description: Try fewer filters or explore the reference graph to find related laws.

home.syncPrefix: The corpus is up to date. Last sync with

empty.title: Nothing new
empty.description: The corpus has no recent publications. Sync with legalize-es to pull the latest changes.

tiles.graphSub: See how laws cite each other

errors.notFoundDescription: Check the URL or go back home.

graph.selectNode: Select a node in the graph to see details.
graph.buildingFirstTime: Building the graph for the first time. This may take a minute or two…

error.title: Couldn't load the subgraph from that law
error.seedMissing: The requested seed law ({{seed}}) isn't in the current graph.
error.trySuggestions: Try one of the most connected laws in the corpus:

kindDesc.law: Law with 169 articles and 1,248 incoming references.
kindDesc.article: Article belonging to Title I, Chapter II.
kindDesc.reference: Supranational law or doctrine cited by the active law.
kindDesc.amendment: Law that amends the active one.
kindDesc.repealed: Repealed law — shown for historical context.
